questions.question: Which among the following committees is related to Non-Banking Finance Companies?
questions.explanation: A C Shah Committee on deposit insurance for non-banking finance companies (NBFCs) has recommended that General Insurance Corporation (GIC) and Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) provide the risk cover.

questions.question: Raja Chelliah Committee is associated with ___________?
questions.explanation: In, 1991, the Government set up the Tax Reforms Committee under the Chairmanship of Raja J. Chelliah to examine the then tax structure of the country.

questions.question: Who among the following is NOT associated with Committee to Review the Working of the Monetary System?
questions.explanation: All 3 were chairmen of the committees that Reviewed the Working of the Monetary System. They were constituted in different periods and their recommendations also differed.

questions.question: Which among the following committees is related to Lead Bank Scheme?
questions.explanation: UK Sharma Committee was tasked to review Lead Bank Scheme.

questions.question: Which among the following committees is related to direct taxes?
questions.explanation: Income Tax Settlement Commission was set up in 1976 by the Central Government on the recommendation of Justice KN Wanchoo Committee.

questions.question: Nariman Committee is related to ________?
questions.explanation: Committee of Bankers on Branch Expansion Programme of public sector banks appointed by Reserve Bank of India under the Chairmanship of Nariman (Nariman Committee) endorsed the idea of area approach in its report (November 1969) recommending that in order to enable the public sector banks to discharge their social responsibilities, each bank should concentrate on certain districts where it should act as a 'Lead Bank'.

questions.question: Which among the following committees is related to Agricultural Finance and Cooperative Societies?
questions.explanation: R S Saria Committee recommended on constitution of agricultural finance and cooperative societies.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT related to working capital of banks?
questions.explanation: In 1995, the Reserve Bank had set up the Committee for Proposing Legislation on Electronic Funds Transfer and other Electronic Payments (Chairperson : K.S.Shere). The Shere Committee had recommended a set of EFT Regulations by the Reserve Bank under the Reserve Bank of India Act,1934 and amendment to the Bankers' Books Evidence Act,1881 as short term measures and promotion of a few Acts like the Electronic Funds Transfer Act, the Computer Misuse and Data Protection Act etc. as long term measures.

questions.question: Which committee recommended improvements in customer services in banks?
questions.explanation: RBI had set up in 1990 a Committee headed by M.N.Goiporia, the then Chairman of SBI. The Committee had made various recommendations to ensure improvement in the customer service in the banks.

questions.question: James Raj Committee is related to ____________?
questions.explanation: The committee made recommendations on functioning of Public Sector Banks. It was set up in 1977 by RBI.

questions.question: Committee under whose chairmanship is related to rural credit from cooperatives?
questions.explanation: The Expert Committee on Rural Credit under the chairmanship of Vyas, observed that cooperatives were free to fix their rates of interest on deposits as well as loans and advances. Cooperatives in most of the states had taken advantage of this relaxation and had hiked the interest rates by as much as 3 percentage points as compared to commercial banks.

questions.question: Which committee recommended privatization of insurance sector?
questions.explanation: In 1993, the Government of India appointed R N Malhotra Committee to lay down a road map for privatisation of the life insurance sector. While the committee submitted its report in 1994, it took another six years before the enabling legislation was passed in the year 2000, legislation amending the Insurance Act of 1938 and legislating the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act of 2000.

questions.question: Nair committee is related to _________?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank constituted the Committee under the chairmanship of Shri. M. V. Nair to re-examine the existing classification and suggest revised guidelines with regard to priority sector lending and related issues.

questions.question: Committee under whose chairmanship studied mutual funds scheme in India?
questions.explanation: In order to examine and finalize the draft regulations for Collective Investment schemes- mutual funds, SEBI appointed this committee under the Chairmanship of Dr. S. A. Dave.

questions.question: Urban cooperative societies were studied by the committee under the chairmanship of ________?
questions.explanation: Committee on Urban Cooperative Banks, 1978 under Madhav Das provided a well-documented study of urban banking sector in India and set standards of viability for sustained growth of urban banks.

questions.question: Narasimham Committee is related to _______?
questions.explanation: The Narasimham-II Committee was tasked with the progress review of the implementation of the banking reforms since 1992 with the aim of further strengthening the financial institutions of India.

questions.question: Committee under whose chairmanship made recommendations on corporate governance?
questions.explanation: Task Force of CII on corporate governance headed by Shri Naresh Chandra made recommendations to enunciate additional principles that can improve corporate governance in spirit and in practice.

questions.question: Committee under the chairmanship of Rekhi is related to ___________.
questions.explanation: Rekhi committee was constituted with the mandate to structure the indirect taxation system.

questions.question: Committee to report on disinvestments in PSU sectors was headed by __________.
questions.explanation: It was headed by G. V. Ramakrishna. It recommended to offload equity in many public-sector undertakings. It was constituted for a period of 3 years.

questions.question: Committee on Revisiting & Revitalising the PPP model of Infrastructure Development was chaired by ___________?
questions.explanation: Committee on Revisiting & Revitalising the PPP model of Infrastructure Development was chaired by Dr. Vijay Kelkar. It suggested the revival of a defunct proposal to establish 3P India to support PPP projects.

questions.question: Abid Hussain Committee was tasked to make reports on ________.
questions.explanation: Abid Hussain, the chairman of the expert committee on small scale enterprises was broadly favouring dereservation of industries. The recommendation was based on the argument that of the items that were reserved, very few were actually manufactured, implying that the continuation of protection was not justified.

questions.question: Committee on New education policy was headed by ____________
questions.explanation: The committee for preparation of final draft of National Education Policy was constituted under the chairmanship of eminent scientist Padma Vibhushan Dr K. Kasturirangan. He will head a nine-member panel formed by the HRD ministry to prepare a new education policy.

questions.question: Committee to study the existing sports governance frame work is chaired by __________.
questions.explanation: Committee to study the existing sports governance frame work, issues relating to sports governance in the country, recent developments related to sports governance, including court judgments & international best practices and make recommendations on bringing out a comprehensive National Sports Development Code across sports disciplines is chaired by Injeti Srinivas.

questions.question: Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR) was set up by the Government of India in the year _________.
questions.explanation: Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR) was set up by the Government of India in 1962. It was chaired by Dr. Vikram Sarabhai for formulating the Indian Space Programme.

questions.question: Committee under whose chairmanship recommended Panchayati Raj System?
questions.explanation: Balwant Rai Mehta Committee was appointed to examine the working of the Community Development Programme and the National Extension Service. It recommended the establishment of the scheme of 'democratic decentralisation' which finally came to be known as Panchayati Raj.

questions.question: Who headed the committee that reported on creation of Telangana issue?
questions.explanation: Committee for Consultations on the Situation in Andhra Pradesh (CCSAP) was headed by former Chief Justice B. N. Srikrishna to look into the demand for separate statehood for Telangana or keep the State united in the present form, Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: Fundamental Duties in the constitution was based on the recommendations of the committee under the chairmanship of __________.
questions.explanation: Committee under the chairmanship of Swaran Singh recommended inclusion of Fundamental duties under Part IV A of the constitution. Most of the recommendations were taken up and added in Article 51A.

questions.question: Which among the following committees is associated with state reorganization?
questions.explanation: All the above committee has made recommendations on state reorganization on linguistic basis. Final reorganization came as a result of recommendations of Fazil Ali committee.

questions.question: Shekatkar Committee made recommendations for reforms in which area?
questions.explanation: Reform initiatives were recommended by a committee headed by Lt Gen DB Shekatkar (retd) which had the mandate to recommend measures for enhancing of combat capability and rebalancing defence expenditure of the armed forces to increase "teeth to tail ratio".

questions.question: Tendulkar Committee on Poverty line was revised by committee headed by ___________.
questions.explanation: The Expert Group under the Chairmanship of Dr. C. Rangarajan to Review the Methodology for Measurement of Poverty in the country was constituted by the Planning Commission in June 2012. It revised the methods adopted by Tendulkar Committee to arrive at poverty line.
